---
title: "Captions & Subtitles"
description: "A guide to generating, styling, and burning-in captions with FrameLane."
---

# Captions & Subtitles

FrameLane can auto-generate subtitles from speech or burn-in captions you provide. This guide covers both workflows.

## Auto-generated captions

Pass a `caption` edit with no transcript — FrameLane runs speech-to-text and produces word-level timed subtitles.

```python
job = client.jobs.create(
    assets=[asset.id],
    edits=[{"type": "caption", "style": "subtitles", "language": "en"}],
    output={"format": "mp4", "resolution": "1080p"},
)
```

## Styling options

| Parameter       | Default      | Options                            |
|-----------------|--------------|------------------------------------|
| `style`         | `subtitles`  | `subtitles`, `karaoke`, `minimal`  |
| `font_size`     | `24`         | Any integer                        |
| `font_color`    | `#FFFFFF`    | Any hex color                      |
| `background`    | `#00000080`  | Any hex color with alpha           |
| `position`      | `bottom`     | `top`, `center`, `bottom`          |

## Providing your own transcript

Supply a `transcript` field with an array of timed segments to skip speech-to-text and burn-in your own text.

```json
{
  "type": "caption",
  "style": "subtitles",
  "transcript": [
    {"start": 0.0, "end": 2.5, "text": "Welcome to the demo."},
    {"start": 2.5, "end": 5.0, "text": "Let's get started."}
  ]
}
```
